title: eriogonum gracile var. polygonoides
intro: Eriogonum gracile var. polygonoides, also known as Mormon tea or Redstem buckwheat, is a shrubby plant in the buckwheat family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as sore throats and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Eriogonum gracile var. polygonoides include its ability to reduce fever, soothe coughs, and act as an expectorant to help clear mucus from the lungs. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water or combining them with a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
